name: Indian IT Sector (TCS/Infosys/Wipro)
summary: Day 49. EXPOSURE INCREASING VIA REMITTANCE + CONSUMER CHANNELS. SMH $453 (+0.2% April 16, +15.0% 1M) AI-driven. NEW CHANNEL: IMF cut 2026 MENA growth to 1.1% from 3.9% — ~8M Indian diaspora in Gulf remit ~$40B/yr to India; Gulf contraction hits remittance-dependent sectors (Kerala banking, Hyderabad/Kochi real estate). Fed rate-cut narrative pushed further out (June → September risk) on CPI 3.3% + UMich 47.6 + inflation expectations 4.8%, compressing US IT client budgets. TSMC Q1 earnings April 16 binary event for broader semi complex. Q4 FY26 earnings underway with mixed guidance.

signals.content: Indian IT index underperforming Nifty. Q4 FY26 earnings weak — Infosys guidance cut, HDFC Bank NIM compression (not IT but signals broader weakness). The crisis-to-IT chain: oil spike → US inflation stays elevated → Fed holds rates → US GDP revised down to 1.8% → enterprise IT spending cuts → Indian IT revenue pressure. SMH (semiconductors) -9.2% monthly. Nifty IT index tracking ~15% below pre-crisis levels. (Sources: Business Standard, yfinance, company filings)

Indian IT sector faces worsening indirect crisis exposure through US recession risk. The transmission chain: oil inflation -> Fed can't cut (52% pricing Fed HIKE by year-end) -> US growth pressure (ISM stagflation signal: Prices 78.3, employment contracting) -> IT client spending cuts. SMH (semiconductors) -1.7% monthly. No specific new IT sector developments but the macro backdrop has deteriorated significantly since March 28. US bond market pricing growth deterioration (10Y yields fell on peace rally day — bonds pricing recession while equities celebrated). Q4 FY26 earnings season approaching — guidance risk elevated. (Sources: yfinance, ISM, CNBC)

Indian IT services sector operating but exposed to (a) US demand slowdown if war drags into Q2 earnings, (b) rupee volatility (Rs 92.93 holding 93). No major pre-warnings yet. Q1 earnings season starts April 10 globally — Indian IT will react to US bank earnings (JPM, GS, MS, Wells, BAC report April 10-15). Watch TCS/Infosys language on US discretionary spending in upcoming results.

Day 45, Ceasefire Day 5. SMH $436.88 (+1.5% Friday, +8.9% 1M). Indian IT sector faces indirect crisis exposure: oil spike → US inflation → Fed can't cut → US growth pressure → IT demand weakness. Q4 FY26 earnings season underway — Infosys and TCS guidance revisions expected. The Islamabad talks failure compounds US recession risk through the oil→inflation→Fed pathway. Helium semiconductor cliff bear case is dead (TrendForce window expired, Samsung LTAs secured). (Source: yfinance, markets.md)

edges.context: 60-70% of Indian IT revenue comes from US clients. S&P weakness + ISM stagflation signal -> enterprise spending cuts -> Indian IT revenue decline.
IT is the largest sector in Nifty by weight (~15%). IT weakness drags the index even if other sectors hold.
Fed rate path determines US growth outlook which determines IT spending. Fed holding/hiking rates = growth pressure = IT headwind. Market now pricing 52% Fed hike probability.

trigger_points.condition: Major Indian IT company issues profit warning citing US demand slowdown
trigger_points.mechanism: Would confirm the crisis cascade reaching Indian IT earnings. Nifty would take a direct hit. Rupee weakens further as IT is India's largest forex earner.
